questions.question: What is the primary goal of CPU scheduling?
options: A) To minimize the response time of a process
B) To maximize the throughput of a system
C) To minimize the overhead of context switching
D) To divide time between multiple tasks
questions.explanation: The primary goal of CPU scheduling is to divide the available CPU time among multiple tasks, ensuring that all tasks receive a fair share of the CPU time.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key consideration in CPU scheduling?
options: A) Process priority
B) Process size
C) Process waiting time
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Process priority is a key consideration in CPU scheduling, as it determines the order in which tasks are executed.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Round-Robin scheduling algorithm?
options: A) To minimize the overhead of context switching
B) To maximize the throughput of a system
C) To divide the available CPU time among multiple tasks in a circular manner
D) To prioritize tasks based on their priority
questions.explanation: The purpose of the Round-Robin scheduling algorithm is to divide the available CPU time among multiple tasks in a circular manner, ensuring that each task receives a fair share of the CPU time.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of the First-Come-First-Served (FCFS) scheduling algorithm?
options: A) To minimize the overhead of context switching
B) To minimize the waiting time of tasks
C) To maximize the throughput of a system
D) To prioritize tasks based on their priority
questions.explanation: The primary advantage of the First-Come-First-Served (FCFS) scheduling algorithm is that it minimizes the waiting time of tasks, as tasks are executed in the order they arrive.

questions.question: What is the purpose of process synchronization?
options: A) To divide the available CPU time among multiple tasks
B) To protect shared resources from multiple processes
C) To prioritize tasks based on their priority
D) To minimize the overhead of context switching
questions.explanation: The purpose of process synchronization is to protect shared resources from multiple processes, ensuring that only one process can access a shared resource at a time.
